title: trifolium procumbens var. lagrangei
intro: Trifolium procumbens var. lagrangei, also known as Lagrange's or creeping white clover, is a perennial herb of the legume family, characterized by its creeping stems and white or pinkish flowers. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its alkaloid content. The health benefits of Trifolium procumbens var. lagrangei include its use in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential in reducing inflammation and improving wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used in traditional medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
